column2: A set of instructions written in the Python programming language that tells the computer what to do.

column1: How does Python execute a program?
column2: Python executes statements one at a time from top to bottom unless directed otherwise.
A single instruction that tells Python to perform an action.
A variable is a named location that stores a value so it can be used later in a program.
A value is a piece of information stored or used by a program.
A data type is an attribute of a value that tells Python how to store

column1: What relationship exists between a variable and a value?
column2: A variable refers to a value.

column1: What happens when a variable is reassigned?
column2: The variable refers to a new value from that point forward in the program.

column1: When does Python evaluate the value of a variable?
column2: When the statement using the variable is executed.

column1: Why does the first print(name) output "Anthony" before name is reassigned?
column2: Because Python executes the first print() before the reassignment statement.

column1: Why does changing one variable not affect another variable?
column2: Each variable stores its own reference to a value and is independent unless explicitly related.

column1: What character does print() append by default?
Why does each print() statement appear on a separate line?
column2: Because print() appends a newline character (\n) after printing by default.

column3: Because they are printed by a single print() call before one newline is appended.

column1: What is the most important execution rule learned in this lesson?
column2: Python executes one statement at a time from top to bottom.

column1: Should derived information usually be stored as a variable?
column2: No. If it can be calculated from existing data
column3: it is usually better to derive it when needed.

column2: The information that represents the current condition of the application while it is running.

column1: What question should you ask before creating a new variable?
column2: Does this information need to be stored
column3: or can it be derived from other information?
